title: Restore floating-point rounding state after numerical tests
prompt:
Repair the order-dependent numerical test failure in this C repository. The rejecting lower-bound case deliberately evaluates under FE_DOWNWARD and treats its callback's nonzero rejection status as an expected result, but rounding_scope_run returns that status before restoring the process-wide rounding mode. A later nearest-tie case then observes the leaked mode and fails. Keep the rounding_scope_run API and callback result contract unchanged. Every path that changes the rounding mode, including a nonzero callback result, must restore the exact mode that was active on entry; do not merely force FE_TONEAREST. Preserve environment-error reporting when changing or restoring the mode fails.

Reproduce the isolated and order-dependent behavior with `bash test.sh direct` and `bash test.sh shuffled 100103`. The complete `bash test.sh` command also checks exact restoration from a non-default caller mode on both the success and callback-rejection paths. The shuffled execution is pinned to seed 100103 and uses a repository-defined deterministic shuffle. Use only the C17 toolchain and standard library. Do not add network access, clocks, sleeps, subprocess-driven test ordering, or unseeded randomness. Do not modify `tests/`, `test.sh`, or the public header.
